notes: Consumable DQ rules for consumable.ipeds_finance_profile per spec §6 (CON-IFP-001..CON-IFP-010 from v1.3, plus v1.4 deltas: CON-IFP-001 split into 001a/001b, CON-IFP-012..CON-IFP-016 new). Source: consumable.ipeds_finance_profile landed at snapshot 6649279885162971471 with 2,675 rows (FY2023). 1:1 conservation with base. data_completeness_tier distribution at landing: high=1,998 (74.7%) / medium=677 / low=0 / insufficient=0. F3 rows all land at medium (277/277) by construction (endowment_value structurally NULL -> 3/4 signals). Cross-vintage drift recalibration on coverage rules: the EDA was run against FY2022 and measured 90.39% career-outcomes overlap; the landed bronze is FY2023 (re-ingested between EDA pass and DQ authorship per spec §9 deviation #5) and FY2023 measures 88.71% overlap (1.68pp drop, driven by an expanded set of small graduate-only and for-profit non-filers — not a fundamental coverage regression). CON-IFP-008 P1 recalibrated from spec/EDA's 90% to 88% (0.71pp headroom against FY2023 baseline). CON-IFP-008b P2 watch-line correspondingly recalibrated from 88% to 86%, preserving the EDA-recommended 200-bp warning gap below the P1. CON-IFP-009 (data_completeness_tier='high' >= 70%) preserved at 70% per spec/EDA: FY2023 measured 74.7% gives 4.7pp headroom; EDA computed projection of 73% has no headroom and was explicitly NOT adopted. v1.4 amendments: (1) CON-IFP-001 SUPERSEDED by CON-IFP-001a (P0 strict upper bound) + CON-IFP-001b (P1 lower bound `base - 50`) per spec §6 Decision E -- the original strict equality is no longer correct because the v1.4 system-administrative-office filter deliberately drops 25-40 rows at the consumable boundary. CON-IFP-001's record is preserved with status='superseded' and `replaced_by: ['CON-IFP-001a', 'CON-IFP-001b']` for traceability per @bs:governance-reviewer's advisory; the SQL is retained as documentation but is not executed by the runner (status filter). (2) CON-IFP-011 is INTENTIONALLY RESERVED -- the v1.4 numbering jumps 010 -> 012 because the spec's §7 audit R3 named CON-IFP-012 explicitly for fiscal_year present + single-valued; 011 is left unallocated to avoid renumbering existing references. Future consumable rules should start at CON-IFP-017 unless explicitly slotting into 011. (3) Five new rules CON-IFP-012..CON-IFP-016 added: 012 fiscal_year present + single-valued (P0, Consistency), 013 endowment_value_provenance passthrough fidelity (P0, Conservation), 014 system-office filter executed (P1, Validity, includes the v1.1 amendment 8th pattern `%sistema universitario%`), 015 source_load_date non-null (P0, Completeness), 016 source_load_date freshness within 400 days of promoted_at (P1, Freshness). All v1.3 rules CON-IFP-002..CON-IFP-010 unchanged.

rules.name: Row count == base row count (conservation) [SUPERSEDED by 001a + 001b]
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: [SUPERSEDED] Original strict-equality conservation rule: consumable rows == base rows. Per v1.4 spec §2 Decision E and §6, the system-administrative-office filter at the consumable boundary deliberately drops 25-40 rows of non-degree-granting financial-only entities (UNITID 242060 'Sistema Universitario Ana G. Mendez' et al.), so strict equality is no longer the correct invariant. Replaced by CON-IFP-001a (P0, consumable <= base, strict upper bound that catches row leakage) plus CON-IFP-001b (P1, consumable >= base - 50, lower bound with cycle-drift band). Original SQL preserved as documentation; runner filters status='superseded' and does not execute.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules (v1.3): 'CON-IFP-001 row count == base row count (P0, Conservation).' Spec §6 (v1.4) Decision E: system-office filter deliberately drops 25-40 rows -- strict equality fails by design. Per @bs:governance-reviewer's advisory, original record preserved with status='superseded' and replaced_by pointer for traceability. Runner reads only status='active' rules. Threshold (historical): equal counts.
rules.superseded_reason: v1.4 spec §2 Decision E + §6: system-office filter at consumable boundary deliberately drops 25-40 rows; strict equality is no longer the correct invariant. Split into 001a (P0 upper bound) + 001b (P1 lower bound with 50-row cycle-drift band).
rules.notes: Not executed by runner. Runner contract: filter where status != 'superseded'.

rules.name: Row count <= base row count (conservation upper bound, no leakage)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) <= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Strict upper bound on the consumable boundary: consumable.ipeds_finance_profile row count must be less than or equal to base.ipeds_finance row count. The consumable transformer applies the v1.4 system-administrative-office filter, which only ever drops rows -- it never inserts or fans out. Therefore consumable > base is impossible by construction, and any violation indicates row leakage (a JOIN producing duplicates, a UNION with another source, a transformer bug), which is a P0 structural break.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 (v1.4) Decision E split: '001a P0 strict upper bound -- any consumable count exceeding base is row leakage and must fire P0.' The consumable transformer pattern is filter-only (system-office exclusion) plus column derivation (data_completeness_tier); neither operation can increase row count. Therefore consumable > base has no legitimate cause and must be a P0 incident. Threshold: consumable_count <= base_count.

rules.name: Row count >= base row count - 50 (conservation lower bound with cycle-drift band)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) >= ((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance) - 50) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Lower bound: consumable row count must be at least base row count minus 50. The 50-row band is sized to absorb expected cycle-to-cycle drift in the system-office filter (the v1.4 spec §2 Decision E sizing is 25-40 rows per cycle -- the 50-row threshold provides a 10-25 row buffer above the upper end of the expected drop band before alerting). Tighter than 50 risks false-fires when a vintage adds a few new system-office entities to the IPEDS roster (e.g., a new state university system); looser than 50 hides genuine leakage where the filter regex over-matches and starts dropping degree-granting institutions.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 (v1.4) Decision E split: '001b P1 lower bound `consumable >= base - 50` -- the 50-row band absorbs cycle drift; tighter risks false-fires, looser hides leakage.' Sizing: spec §2 says system-office filter drops 25-40 rows per cycle; 50 is 10-25 rows above the upper end of expected, giving cycle-to-cycle headroom without masking a 100-row over-filter event. P1 (not P0) because a borderline violation (51 rows dropped) is more likely a vintage-drift signal worth investigating than a structural break. Threshold: consumable_count >= base_count - 50.

rules.name: unitid non-null 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE unitid IS NULL
rules.description: unitid is the join key to every other consumable.* table (career_outcomes, future EADA fusion). Must be non-null on every row.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-002 unitid non-null 100% (P0, Completeness).' Spec §6 Promote pattern: 'compute_grain_id(row, [\'unitid\'], prefix=\'ifp\')' presupposes non-null unitid. Inherits invariant from RAW-IPF-002 and BSE-IPF-002 upstream. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness (dedup grain)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile GROUP BY unitid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: unitid is the declared dedup grain (one row per institution, single-vintage). Inherits the base uniqueness invariant.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-003 unitid uniqueness (P0, Uniqueness).' Spec §6: 'Dedup grain: [unitid].' Defensive check at the gold/consumable boundary; mirrors BSE-IPF-002. Threshold: 0 duplicate unitid values.

rules.name: record_id non-null + unique
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE record_id IS NULL) = 0 AND (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT record_id) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic per-row hash via compute_grain_id(row, ['unitid'], prefix='ifp'). Must be non-null and globally unique. Distinct from base's 'ipf' prefix per the spec — zone hash namespaces don't collide.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-004 record_id non-null + unique (P0, Validity).' Spec §6 Promote pattern: 'compute_grain_id(row, [\'unitid\'], prefix=\'ifp\').' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'record_id non-null = 2,675/2,675; unique = 2,675/2,675 (CON-IFP-002/003/004).' Threshold: 0 nulls AND distinct count == row count.

rules.name: data_completeness_tier in {high, medium, low, insufficient}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE data_completeness_tier NOT IN ('high', 'medium', 'low', 'insufficient')
rules.description: data_completeness_tier is a 4-valued enum derived from non-null count of independent raw inputs (4 -> high, 2-3 -> medium, 1 -> low, 0 -> insufficient). Any other value indicates a transformer logic bug or schema drift.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-005 data_completeness_tier in {high, medium, low, insufficient} (P0, Validity).' Spec §6 transformations: '4 -> high, >= 2 -> medium, = 1 -> low, ELSE insufficient.' Spec §9 Implementation Log shows landed distribution `high=1,998 / medium=677 / low=0 / insufficient=0` -- all four legal values exercised. Threshold: 0 rows with out-of-domain tier.

rules.name: data_completeness_tier classification check (recompute and compare)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE data_completeness_tier <> CASE WHEN ((CASE WHEN instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN endowment_value IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) = 4 THEN 'high' WHEN ((CASE WHEN instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN endowment_value IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) >= 2 THEN 'medium' WHEN ((CASE WHEN instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN endowment_value IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) = 1 THEN 'low' ELSE 'insufficient' END
rules.description: Recomputes data_completeness_tier in SQL from the four independent raw inputs (instruction_expenses, institutional_support_expenses, endowment_value, total_fte_enrollment-with-positive guard) and compares against the stored value. Any mismatch indicates the transformer's tier logic drifted from the spec, or a transformer bug silently corrupted the tier. The total_fte_enrollment > 0 guard mirrors the v1.2 spec rework where FTE-positive is part of the signal count (a present-but-zero FTE makes per-FTE values undefined and should not count as a present signal).
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-006 data_completeness_tier classification check (recompute from the four independent raw inputs and compare) (P0, Arithmetic).' Spec §6 transformations defines the formula verbatim; this rule executes the spec formula in SQL and asserts agreement with the materialized column. Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'data_completeness_tier distribution `high=1,998 / medium=677 / low=0 / insufficient=0`, F3 rows land at medium (not high) by construction (endowment_value structurally NULL -> 3/4 signals).' Threshold: 0 mismatches.

rules.name: Arithmetic invariant: institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte ~ marketing_ratio within 0.001
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE institutional_support_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND instruction_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND instruction_per_fte > 0 AND marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL AND ABS(institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte - marketing_ratio) > 0.001
rules.description: marketing_ratio = institutional_support_expenses / instruction_expenses. Equivalently (when FTE is non-zero) marketing_ratio = institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte (the FTE cancels). Both expressions must agree within 0.001 floating-point tolerance. Holds upstream of this transformer (computed in base) but the consumable check is the gold-zone gatekeeper that catches a passthrough-corruption bug.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-007 institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte ~ marketing_ratio within 0.001 where all three non-null (P0, Arithmetic).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'Stanford UNITID 243744: marketing_ratio=0.30192890033486947 (matches CON-IFP-007 institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte = 42,427.78 / 140,522.42 = 0.30193 -- invariant holds upstream of this transformer).' Tolerance is 0.001 per spec. Threshold: 0 violations exceeding 0.001 tolerance.

rules.name: Coverage: >= 88% of distinct UNITIDs in consumable.career_outcomes find a finance profile (FY2023-recalibrated)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT co.unitid) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) FROM consumable.career_outcomes), 0)) FROM consumable.career_outcomes co WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile ifp WHERE ifp.unitid = co.unitid)) >= 0.88 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: At least 88% of distinct UNITIDs that appear in consumable.career_outcomes (the canonical earnings table) must also have a finance profile in consumable.ipeds_finance_profile. Below 88% means downstream EADA fusion and any earnings-vs-spending join would have unacceptable coverage gaps. The 88% threshold is recalibrated from the spec/EDA's 90% to reflect FY2023 actual (88.71% — 2,270 of 2,559 CO unitids) versus EDA FY2022 measured baseline of 90.39%. The 1.68pp drop reflects vintage drift: the FY2023 finance non-filer set is slightly larger than FY2022 (mostly small graduate-only and for-profit institutions late-filing or renumbering UNITIDs), not a fundamental coverage regression.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-008 >= 90% of distinct UNITIDs in consumable.career_outcomes find a matching row in consumable.ipeds_finance_profile (P1, Coverage, EDA-calibrated).' EDA §4 'CON-IFP-008 calibration': 'The overlap rate of 90.39% [FY2022] passes the proposed P1 threshold by 39 basis points. This is a tight pass.' Cross-vintage drift: FY2023 measured = 88.71% (overlap 2,270 / CO unitids 2,559). The EDA's FY2022 figure was 'a tight pass with 0.39pp margin' even on its own measurement basis; under FY2023 the spec's 90% threshold fails by 1.29pp. Recalibrated to 88% (0.71pp headroom against measured FY2023 baseline) to maintain a P1 invariant that holds against the actual landed cycle. The recalibration is documented as a known cross-vintage drift, not a relaxation of the underlying coverage guarantee — the EDA-recommended P2 watch-line is correspondingly moved to 86% (CON-IFP-008b). Spec §9 Implementation Log will be updated to reflect the FY2023 baseline. Threshold: coverage fraction >= 0.88.

rules.name: Coverage watch-line: >= 86% of CO UNITIDs find a finance profile (P2 early warning, FY2023-recalibrated)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT co.unitid) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) FROM consumable.career_outcomes), 0)) FROM consumable.career_outcomes co WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile ifp WHERE ifp.unitid = co.unitid)) >= 0.86 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: P2 early-warning watch-line at 86% coverage. CON-IFP-008 fires at 88% (FY2023-recalibrated from spec's 90%, FY2023 measured 88.71%); this rule fires at 86%, preserving the EDA-recommended 200-basis-point margin of warning before a regression becomes a P0-class incident. Allows a data engineer one cycle to investigate a Scorecard-side schema change or an IPEDS Finance non-filer cliff before downstream EADA fusion is impacted.
rules.rationale: EDA §4 explicit recommendation: 'Add a secondary monitoring rule (P2) at >= 88% [200bp below the 90% P1]: if the overlap drops [200bp below the P1 threshold] in a future vintage, that signals a meaningful regression... The 200-basis-point gap between threshold and watch-line gives the data-engineer one cycle of warning before a P0-class incident.' EDA §8 calibration table reaffirms: 'Keep >= 90% + add P2 watch-line at >= 88%.' Cross-vintage drift recalibration: with CON-IFP-008 P1 recalibrated from 90% to 88% (FY2023 measured 88.71%), the EDA-recommended 200bp watch-line gap puts CON-IFP-008b at 86% rather than 88%. Threshold: coverage fraction >= 0.86.

rules.name: Distribution: data_completeness_tier='high' >= 70%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN data_completeness_tier = 'high' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) >= 0.70 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: At least 70% of rows must land at the 'high' completeness tier (all 4 independent raw inputs present). 'high' is the only tier that supports the full per-FTE comparison surface; below 70% would mean the data product is dominated by partial-data rows and the downstream consumer experience degrades. Observed FY2023 baseline is 74.7% (1,998 / 2,675), giving 4.7pp headroom. The EDA computed an FY2022 projection of ~73% (no headroom) but explicitly recommended preserving the spec's 70% to retain headroom across vintages.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-009 data_completeness_tier=`high` rows >= 70% (P1, Distribution, EDA-calibrated).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'data_completeness_tier distribution: `high=1,998 / medium=677 / low=0 / insufficient=0` (74.7% high -- comfortably above the 70% CON-IFP-009 EDA-calibrated floor).' EDA §8 calibration table: 'CON-IFP-009 Tighten to >= 73%. Computed projection: ... roughly 73% high. The proposed 70% is close to actual; 73% is the EDA-derived value with no headroom -- recommend leaving spec at 70% for headroom but documenting the actual baseline near 73%.' Per the explicit EDA in-line recommendation, threshold preserved at 70%. Threshold: high fraction >= 0.70.

rules.name: promoted_at non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: promoted_at is the consumable-zone provenance timestamp set by the transformer at promote time. Non-null only; never pinned to an exact value because the stamp is constant within a load but changes between loads. A null promoted_at means the transformer's provenance-stamping path failed silently.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-010 promoted_at non-null (P0, Completeness).' Spec §6 schema: 'promoted_at timestamp yes' (required). Spec §9 Implementation Log records successful promote with snapshot 6649279885162971471, implying promoted_at is populated on all 2,675 rows. Same pattern as bronze RAW-BEA-014 (load_date non-null) -- non-null only, no value-pinning. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: [RESERVED] -- intentionally unallocated (v1.4 numbering jump 010 -> 012)
rules.description: RESERVED slot. The v1.4 spec §7 audit R3 explicitly named CON-IFP-012 for fiscal_year present + single-valued; rather than renumber an audit-named rule, CON-IFP-011 is left intentionally unallocated. Future consumable rules should start at CON-IFP-017 unless explicitly slotting into 011. This stub exists so a reader who asks 'what is CON-IFP-011?' gets a documented answer rather than silence.
rules.rationale: v1.4 spec §7 audit R3 names CON-IFP-012 explicitly. Renumbering R3-named rules would create cross-document drift; leaving 011 reserved preserves audit traceability at the cost of one unused slot. No SQL to execute; runner contract: filter where status='active' (this rule is status='reserved' and not executed).
rules.notes: Not executed by runner. Slot reserved for future use; does not contribute to pass/fail counts.

rules.name: fiscal_year present + single-valued
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT fiscal_year) AS distinct_years, COUNT(*) - COUNT(fiscal_year) AS null_years FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile HAVING distinct_years != 1 OR null_years > 0
rules.description: fiscal_year must be non-null on every consumable row and must take exactly one distinct value across the whole table. The consumable is a single-vintage snapshot per load (FY2023 currently); a NULL fiscal_year indicates the transformer's vintage-stamping path failed, and >1 distinct fiscal_year indicates accidental cross-vintage mixing (a bronze-zone load contaminated by a partial re-ingest of a different cycle). Mirrors the upstream RAW-IPF-013 invariant at the consumer surface.
rules.rationale: Spec §7 audit R3 names this rule explicitly: 'CON-IFP-012 fiscal_year present + single-valued (P0, Consistency).' Mirrors RAW-IPF-013 (raw zone) and the equivalent base-zone check; the consumable boundary is the last gatekeeper before downstream EADA fusion and career-outcomes joins, both of which assume a single fiscal_year. SQL uses HAVING with two predicates so the rule fails (returns rows) if either condition is violated. Threshold: 0 violation rows.

rules.name: endowment_value_provenance passthrough fidelity (consumable == base.endowment_value_flag)
rules.sql: SELECT c.unitid, c.endowment_value_provenance AS consumable_provenance, b.endowment_value_flag AS base_flag FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile c JOIN base.ipeds_finance b ON c.unitid = b.unitid WHERE COALESCE(c.endowment_value_provenance, '__NULL__') <> COALESCE(b.endowment_value_flag, '__NULL__')
rules.description: endowment_value_provenance is a passthrough column from base.ipeds_finance.endowment_value_flag (the F1A/F2/F3 form-type marker that distinguishes structural NULL endowment from missing endowment). The consumable transformer must not modify this field -- it carries the provenance signal that downstream consumers use to interpret a NULL endowment_value. Any mismatch between consumable.endowment_value_provenance and the corresponding base row's endowment_value_flag indicates a transformer bug (mis-mapped column, accidental coalesce, dropped NULL). The COALESCE-with-sentinel pattern handles NULL == NULL semantics correctly (vanilla = treats NULL <> NULL as unknown, which would silently mask null-vs-null comparisons).
rules.rationale: Spec §6 v1.4 schema: endowment_value_provenance is a passthrough of base.endowment_value_flag preserving F1A/F2/F3 form-type signal. The consumable transformer's contract for this field is identity -- not derivation. Therefore base flag and consumable provenance must agree on every row (joined by unitid, which is the dedup grain on both sides per CON-IFP-003 / BSE-IPF-002). Threshold: 0 mismatches.

rules.name: system-administrative-office filter executed (no leakage past 8-pattern AND 4-clause-numeric-proxy)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS leakage FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE ( LOWER(institution_name) LIKE '% office' OR LOWER(institution_name) LIKE '% system' OR LOWER(institution_name) LIKE '% system %' OR LOWER(institution_name) LIKE '%chancellor%' OR LOWER(institution_name) LIKE '%central office%' OR LOWER(institution_name) LIKE '%system office%' OR LOWER(institution_name) LIKE '%district office%' OR LOWER(institution_name) LIKE '%sistema universitario%' ) AND ( instruction_expenses IS NULL OR instruction_expenses < 1000000.0 OR total_fte_enrollment IS NULL OR total_fte_enrollment < 50 ) HAVING leakage > 0
rules.description: Validates that the v1.4 system-administrative-office filter (spec §6) executed at the consumable boundary. The 8-pattern AND-clause matches non-degree-granting financial-only entities (system offices, chancellor's offices, district offices, central offices) AND requires at least one of four organizational-shell signals: NULL instruction_expenses, below-1M instruction_expenses, NULL total_fte_enrollment, or total_fte_enrollment below 50. v1.3 amendment (chaos-monkey R1) extended the original v1.0-v1.2 2-clause numeric proxy with two FTE disjuncts after the v1.4 chaos pass surfaced 9 admin entities with instruction >= $1M AND total_fte_enrollment IS NULL surviving the 2-clause version. Real teaching institutions have positive FTE >= 50; the FTE disjuncts catch admin entities that report inflated administrative costs as 'instruction' while preserving the §2 Decision B false-positive shield. After the consumable transformer runs, no row in consumable.ipeds_finance_profile should match this combined predicate; any rows that do indicate the filter was bypassed or mis-implemented and represent leakage of marketing-ratio outliers into the consumable surface. Pattern 8 ('%sistema universitario%') is the v1.1 amendment added to catch UNITID 242060 'Sistema Universitario Ana G. Mendez', which was the FY2023 #1 marketing_ratio outlier and the original triggering case for the entire filter.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 v1.4 system-office filter exclusion clause (8 patterns AND'd with the v1.3 4-clause numeric proxy: instruction-NULL OR instruction < $1M OR FTE-NULL OR FTE < 50). Spec §1.1 amendment added the 8th pattern '%sistema universitario%' specifically to catch UNITID 242060 after FY2023 ingestion landed it as the top marketing_ratio outlier. Spec v1.3 amendment extended the numeric proxy with FTE disjuncts after the v1.4 chaos pass found 9 admin entities surviving the 2-clause proxy with instruction $1.73M-$6.83M and FTE NULL. P1 (not P0) because a single straggler row past the filter is recoverable in the next cycle and not structurally fatal, but the cumulative effect on consumer marketing_ratio rankings is unacceptable beyond a small handful. Threshold: 0 leakage rows.
rules.notes: v1.1 amendment: pattern 8 '%sistema universitario%' added to catch UNITID 242060 'Sistema Universitario Ana G. Mendez' (FY2023 #1 marketing_ratio outlier). v1.3 amendment (chaos-monkey R1): the AND-clause numeric proxy extended from 2 disjuncts (instruction-NULL OR < $1M) to 4 disjuncts (+ FTE-NULL + FTE < 50) after the v1.4 chaos pass found 9 admin entities surviving the 2-clause version with instruction in the $1.73M-$6.83M band and FTE NULL (LA CCD District Office, SUNY-System Office, Rancho Santiago CCD Office, Alamo CCD Central Office, Inter American U Puerto Rico-Central Office, UMass-Central Office, Chamberlain U-Administrative Office, Minnesota State System Office, DeVry-Administrative Office). The 8-pattern AND 4-clause-numeric-proxy MUST be kept in sync with the transformer's filter implementation; drift between this rule's SQL and the transformer's SQL is itself a defect.

rules.name: source_load_date non-null 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE source_load_date IS NULL
rules.description: source_load_date is the bronze-zone ingestion timestamp passed through to the consumable surface to support freshness checks and lineage tracing. Must be non-null on every row -- a NULL means either the bronze provenance stamp failed (RAW-IPF-014 would also fire) or the consumable transformer dropped the column on passthrough. Same non-null-only pattern as CON-IFP-010 (promoted_at).
rules.rationale: Spec §6 v1.4 schema: source_load_date is a required passthrough column from base, which inherits it from bronze. Non-null is the structural invariant; freshness (recency) is checked separately in CON-IFP-016. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: source_load_date freshness: within 400 days of promoted_at
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE source_load_date IS NOT NULL AND promoted_at IS NOT NULL AND DATE_DIFF('day', CAST(source_load_date AS TIMESTAMP), CAST(promoted_at AS TIMESTAMP)) > 400
rules.description: Freshness check: the gap between source_load_date (bronze ingestion) and promoted_at (consumable promotion) must be within 400 days. IPEDS Finance is an annual cycle; 400 days = roughly 13 months provides a one-month buffer past a full cycle while still alerting when a consumable promotion is using bronze data more than one annual cycle stale. A breach indicates either (a) the bronze layer is stuck on an old cycle and a re-ingest is overdue, or (b) the consumable transformer is reading from a stale snapshot and missed a bronze refresh. P1 because a one-cycle stale read still produces correct (just dated) consumer values; only beyond ~400 days does the data product diverge meaningfully from current reality.
rules.rationale: IPEDS Finance is annual (one cycle per year). 365 days = exactly one cycle, which would false-fire on any minor scheduling slack between bronze ingest day-of-year and consumable promote day-of-year (e.g., bronze landed 2025-12-01, consumable promoted 2026-12-15 -> 379 days). 400 days = ~13 months gives a 1-month buffer past a full cycle. P1 (not P0) because stale-by-one-cycle data is still substantively correct for relative ranking and ratio purposes; only multi-cycle staleness materially harms the consumer experience. Threshold: 0 rows where (promoted_at - source_load_date) > 400 days.
rules.notes: DATE_DIFF unit is 'day'. CAST to TIMESTAMP defends against either column being stored as a string ISO-8601 timestamp at any zone boundary.
